citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
Daily observation of two rabbits to assess their health and well-being is not being done adequately, nor is there direct and
frequent communication with the attending veterinarian regarding their health problems. Two rabbits, identified during
previous inspections with animal welfare concerns, were found with further progression of unresolved medical issues, as
well as new issues that have not been noticed by the licensee, and therefore, have not been re-evaluated by a
veterinarian.
• The tan and white rabbit was found in thin body condition hiding in a hutch by itself. Its ribs were easily felt when
touching its back or sides. This rabbit was again found with its right ear dropped, and the entire inner surface still coated
with thick, layered flaky gray material. This material is also still present below the ear and onto the head but now extends
over the back of the neck as well. The skin below the ear and at the back of the neck is hairless and pink with flaking
grayish tan crust like material. The skin around the thick material along the edges of the ear is hairless and thickened. The
entire ear appeared even further macerated, shrunken, misshaped, and irregular, with marked thickening around the ear
canal opening. The left ear canal still has a similarly thick layered gray crusty material extending from the ear canal but
now goes up most of the length of the inner ear flap.
• The tan rabbit’s left eye was only held partially open upon this inspection with some white and yellowish discharge at the
corner as well as some pink skin surrounding the eye. This rabbit again has matted fur over most of its lower back with
dry, flaky skin. A large area of dark brown, sticky material was still adhered to the fur in this region. Also, this rabbit is still
occasionally shaking its head and has an overall unkempt appearance. Additionally, both ear canals not only contained
thick, flaky gray material again, but now in the right ear, it extends up about half way up the inner ear while the left ear still
has it at mostly at the ear canal opening.
Failure to identify the worsening of pre-existing health issues and failing to maintain timely communication about those
findings with the attending veterinarian can lead to treatment delay, which may negatively impact the health and well-
being of the animals.
Each exhibitor shall establish and maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that include daily observation of all
animals to assess their health and well-being as well as direct and frequent communication so that timely and accurate
information on problems of animal health, behavior, and well-being is conveyed to the attending veterinarian.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
Additional Inspectors:
KRISTIN NAPOLI, SUPERVISORY ANIMAL CARE SPECIALIST n

narrative:
2.40(b)(3)  Direct Repeat
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
Daily observation of two rabbits to assess their health and well-being is not being done adequately, nor is there direct and 
frequent communication with the attending veterinarian regarding their health problems. Two rabbits, identified during 
previous inspections with animal welfare concerns, were found with further progression of unresolved medical issues, as 
well as new issues that have not been noticed by the licensee, and therefore, have not been re-evaluated by a 
veterinarian. 
 • The tan and white rabbit was found in thin body condition hiding in a hutch by itself. Its ribs were easily felt when 
touching its back or sides. This rabbit was again found with its right ear dropped, and the entire inner surface still coated 
with thick, layered flaky gray material. This material is also still present below the ear and onto the head but now extends 
over the back of the neck as well. The skin below the ear and at the back of the neck is hairless and pink with flaking

entire ear appeared even further macerated, shrunken, misshaped, and irregular, with marked thickening around the ear

Failure to identify the worsening of pre-existing health issues and failing to maintain timely communication about those

Each exhibitor shall establish and maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that include daily observation of all

information on problems of animal health, behavior, and well-being is conveyed to the attending veterinarian.

This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee. 
 
Additional Inspectors: 
KRISTIN NAPOLI, SUPERVISORY ANIMAL CARE SPECIALIST n
